title: Huberantha forbesii
taxonomy: Huberantha forbesii is classified under the family Annonaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Huberantha. The classification further breaks down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the phylum Magnoliophyta and then the class Magnoliopsida. The order is Magnoliales, and the family is Annonaceae. intro: Huberantha forbesii, also known as the Star of Bethlehem, is a flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. Traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, the plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Huberantha forbesii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as annonacin and uvaricin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Huberantha forbesii is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and ointments to harness its therapeutic benefits.
